channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: What's fueling Costco's gold rush?
item.description: Move over, gas and hot dogs. Costco shoppers are snatching up the store's 1-ounce gold bars despite the market's lofty price for the precious metal.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, KTLA 5

item.title: FEMA administrator continues pushback against false claims as Helene death toll hits 230
item.description: The head of the U.S. disaster response agency continued to forcefully push back Monday against false claims and conspiracy theories about her agency’s response to Hurricane Helene as the death toll from the storm continued to climb.
item.dc:creator: JOHN RABY, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: As Milton takes aim at Florida, why is Tampa Bay so vulnerable to hurricanes?
item.description: The last time the eye of a major storm like Hurricane Milton struck Tampa Bay, in 1921, the city was a sleepy backwater of a few hundred thousand people.
item.dc:creator: TERRY SPENCER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The mayor of a state capital in Mexico is killed less than 1 week after he took office
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — The mayor of a state capital in southern Mexico has been killed just one week after he took office, officials said Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: California 7-Eleven clerk brutally beaten in series of 'flash mob' attacks to hit Los Angeles-area stores
item.description: A California 7-Eleven store owner was attacked during a "flash mob" robbery on Sunday. "Flash mob" robberies have been occurring throughout the summer since July.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: If you bought this ice cream, you could be eligible for a payout
item.description: If you've enjoyed vanilla-flavored Breyers ice cream over the last eight years, you may be in line for a sweet treat: a cash refund. As KTLA sister station WGN reports, Breyers is paying nearly $9 million to settle a class-action lawsuit filed against Conopco, Inc., and Unilever United States, Inc.
item.dc:creator: Alonzo Small, KTLA 5

item.title: Vigils, protests around the world commemorate the anniversary of Hamas attack on Israel
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Commemorations and protests unfolded across the world on Monday to mark the anniversary of the Oct.
item.dc:creator: DAVID R. MARTIN, TASSANEE VEJPONGSA and JUSTIN SPIKE, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: James Taylor, Luke Combs, Eric Church to take part in Helene relief concert
item.description: All proceeds from the "Concert for Carolina," go to Hurricane Helene relief efforts across the Carolina region, according to an announcement.
item.dc:creator: Jesse Ullmann, NewsNation

item.title: Chicago mayor names new school board after entire panel resigns amid a fight over district control
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Chicago Mayor Brandon Johnson named members of a new school board on Monday, batting away criticism of the move days after all seven members resigned amid an escalating fight over control of the public schools in the nation’s third-largest city.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIA TAREEN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Watch: Aircrew rescues 2 hunters from remote California mountainside
item.description: According to RSO, the two individuals, who were hunting at the time, were experiencing heat-related injuries and were unable to continue their expedition.
item.dc:creator: Will Conybeare, NewsNation

item.title: Are hurricanes intensifying more rapidly than ever before?
item.description: Rapid intensification is defined by the National Hurricane Center as an increase of at least 30 knots (about 35 mph) in maximum sustained winds within a 24-hour period, which can mean a jump of two categories on the Saffir-Simpson scale.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, WGN 9

item.title: Singer Cissy Houston, mother of Whitney Houston, dies at 91
item.description: Cissy Houston, the mother of the late Whitney Houston and a two-time Grammy winner who performed alongside superstar musicians like Elvis Presley, and Aretha Franklin, has died. She was 91.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Many loathe public speakerphone calls, so why do people do it?
item.description: The fact that it's a phone conversation — not just in-person chit-chat — could be part of the reason people find loud speakerphone calls so grating, speculated Ozlem Ayduk.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, NewsNation

item.title: Salmon swim freely in the Klamath River for 1st time in a century after dams removed
item.description: For the first time in more than a century, salmon are swimming freely along the Klamath River and its tributaries near the California-Oregon line.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Feds might use Greensboro immigrant facility to house displaced Helene survivors, Rep. Kathy Manning says
item.description: "The federal government has a contract to use that influx care center," Rep. Kathy Manning said. "It was a former boarding school.
item.dc:creator: Celeste Smith, NewsNation

item.title: James Taylor, Luke Combs, Eric Church to take part in Helene relief concert in NC
item.description: "Concert for Carolina," featuring North Carolina natives James Taylor, Luke Combs and Eric Church, will be held at Bank of America Stadium in Charlotte with all proceeds going to Hurricane Helene relief efforts across the Carolina region, organizers announced.
item.dc:creator: Jesse Ullmann, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Al Pacino claims he almost died of COVID: 'I was gone'
item.description: During a podcast appearance on The New York Times’ “The Interview," Al Pacino claims he once nearly died of COVID.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KRON4

item.title: Florida doctor arrested, accused of running deadly 'pill mill'
item.description: The investigation started after several pharmacists complained about the large amounts of oxycodone being prescribed.
item.dc:creator: Hannah O'Gara, NewsNation

item.title: President Biden approves Florida Emergency Declaration
item.description: President Joe Biden has declared a state of emergency in Florida and ordered Federal assistance to supplement State and local response efforts.
item.dc:creator: Garrett Phillips, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Oklahoma defends Bibles-in-schools proposal
item.description: The two Bibles that Trump has endorsed, We The People Bible and God Bless the U.S.A. Bible, both meet the criteria.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, NewsNation

item.title: Tips, overtime, Social Security: A look at Donald Trump's no-tax pledges and what they might cost
item.description: Donald Trump has pledged to end taxes on everything from tips to Social Security and overtime pay if he's elected to the White House again. But he hasn't detailed how he would fund those ideas and avoid creating a huge budget shortfall, beyond arguing he will usher in an economic boom.
item.dc:creator: MEG KINNARD, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Supreme Court won't hear arguments in Texas emergency abortion case
item.description: The Supreme Court decided not to hear arguments in a case involving Texas that could have provided an answer about whether a state abortion ban conflicts with a federal emergency care law.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump intends to use military to round up opponents: Michael Cohen
item.description: “The big warning that I want people to understand is when Donald Trump says something, stop sane washing it. Stop trying to make it into something which has some normalcy to it. What he says, he intends to do.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Colleges brace for Oct. 7 anniversary
item.description: Colleges face a major test Monday as pro-Palestinian and pro-Israel students both hold events for the first anniversary of Hamas’s attack on Israel.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, KRON4

item.title: US spends a record $17.9 billion on military aid to Israel since last Oct. 7
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The United States has spent a record of at least $17.9 billion on military aid to Israel since the war in Gaza began and led to escalating conflict around the Middle East, according to a report for Brown University's Costs of War project, released Monday on the anniversary of Hamas
item.dc:creator: ELLEN KNICKMEYER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: GOP senator calls Trump special counsel filing 'a temper tantrum from a deranged fanatic'
item.description: Arkansas Sen. Tom Cotton (R) said in a Sunday interview that a recently unsealed filing from special counsel Jack Smith is “a temper tantrum from a deranged fanatic.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: New Jersey high school allegedly banned yellow ribbons honoring Israeli hostages: 'Deeply offensive'
item.description: Yellow ribbons meant to honor Israeli hostages abducted by Hamas were allegedly banned at a club fair, which critics called "deeply offensive" and "blatant antisemitism.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Former model recalls Jeffrey Epstein abuse at private island, speaks out after his death: 'My life spiraled'
item.description: Lisa Phillips is a former model who said late financier Jeffrey Epstein abused her at his private island. The convicted sex offender took his life in 2019 while he was behind bars.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Body language expert says Laundrie parents' first police encounter fair to question – but with major caveat
item.description: Body language expert Susan Constantine breaks down the newly released bodycam video showing Brian Laundrie's parents the night Gabby Petito was reported missing.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: New analysis suggests national debt could increase under Harris, but it would surge under Trump
item.description: No one is likely to be happy with the projected higher deficits laid out in a new analysis of Kamala Harris’ and Donald Trump’s economic plans.
item.dc:creator: Josh Boak, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Dodger Stadium fans toss balls and trash on field, interrupt Padres' 10-2 win that evens NLDS
item.description: Fans threw baseballs in the direction of San Diego left fielder Jurickson Profar and then tossed trash that caused a lengthy delay before the Padres beat the Los Angeles Dodgers 10-2 on Sunday night to even their NL Division Series at a game apiece.
item.dc:creator: BETH HARRIS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Walmart will be closed on Thanksgiving Day for 5th consecutive year
item.description: Walmart will be closed on Thanksgiving Day for the fifth year in a row, the retail giant recently announced in its holiday plans.
item.dc:creator: Kyler Swaim, WFLA, NBC

item.title: North Carolina first responders exchange vows among chaos in Hurricane Helene aftermath
item.description: A North Carolina first responder couple honored their love for each other with a commitment ceremony on Oct. 2 after Hurricane Helene robbed them of their initial wedding plans.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Tennessee nurse and his dog died trying to save a man from floods driven by Helene
item.description: “I’ve never had anyone risk their life for me,” David Boutin, the man Boone McCrary had set out to rescue said. “From what I hear that was the way he always been. He’s my guardian angel, that’s for sure.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Elon Musk makes appearance at Trump rally casting election in dire terms
item.description: (AP) - Billionaire tech executive Elon Musk cast the upcoming presidential election in dire terms during a Saturday appearance with Donald Trump, calling the Republican presidential nominee the only candidate “to preserve democracy in America.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Israel expands its bombardment in Lebanon as thousands flee widening war
item.description: MASNAA BORDER CROSSING, Lebanon (AP) — Powerful new explosions rocked Beirut's southern suburbs late Saturday as Israel expanded its bombardment in Lebanon, also striking a Palestinian refugee camp deep in the north for the first time as it targeted both Hezbollah and Hamas fighters.
item.dc:creator: BASSAM HATOUM, MELANIE LIDMAN and BASSEM MROUE, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Springsteen calls Trump 'most dangerous candidate for president in my lifetime,' backs Harris
item.description: The rock legend said the former president “doesn’t understand the meaning of this country, its history or what it means to be deeply American.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, Fox 5
